samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller.

The method of empty and full judgment is to generate empty and full signals by comparing the Gray code. Use 4-bit Gray code as a read/write pointer for a FIFO with depth 8. The gray code is converted to a four-digit binary number, using the lower three digits of the binary number as the address to access RAM. When the read and write Pointers are equal, the FIFO is null.

When the write pointer has one more cycle RAM than the read pointer, the highest and second-highest bits of the read and write pointer are opposite, the remaining bits are the same, and the FIFO is full. Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations. Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations. Active-low reset signal for both read and write operations. Write increment signal. Triggered when a new data is written into the FIFO. Read increment signal. Triggered when data is read from the FIFO. Write data input. Data to be written into the FIFO. Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations. Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations. Read data output. Data read from the FIFO. Width of the data input and output. Depth of the FIFO.

Use dual-port RAM module to store data.

Use 4-bit Gray code as a read/write pointer for a FIFO with depth DEPTH. The gray code is converted to a four-digit binary number, using the lower $clog2(DEPTH) bits of the binary number as the address to access RAM.

When the read and write Pointers are equal, the FIFO is null. The module includes buffer registers (wptr_buff and rptr_buff) to hold the previous values of the write and read pointers. The buffer registers are updated on the positive edge of the respective clocks and reset to 0 on the respective resets (~wrstn and ~rrstn).

The method of empty and full judgment is to generate empty and full signals by comparing the Gray code. When the read and write pointers are equal, the FIFO is null. The module includes buffer registers (wptr_buff and rptr_buff) to hold the previous values of the write and read pointers. The buffer registers are updated on the positive edge of the respective clocks and reset to 0 on the respective resets (~wrstn and ~rrstn).

The full signal is generated by comparing the current write pointer (wptr) and the synchronized read pointer (rptr_syn). If the highest bit of the write pointer is equal to the bitwise negation of the highest bit of the synchronized read pointer, the module sets the full signal to 1. Otherwise, the full signal is set to 0.

The empty signal is generated by comparing the current read pointer (rptr) and the synchronized write pointer (wptr_syn). If the highest bit of the read pointer is equal to the bitwise negation of the highest bit of the synchronized write pointer, the module sets the empty signal to 1. Otherwise, the empty signal is set to 0.
